notes: LEAGUE BULLETIN: The Ghent Whistlepigs are relocating to Warren, Ohio, effective immediately. Luke Pontzer and the Whistlepigs organization have planted their flag in the Mahoning Valley, and the CBA map has been updated accordingly. We wish them nothing but the best — they're going to need it.

For the historically minded, Warren, Ohio sits squarely in the heart of Mahoning Valley baseball country. The Mahoning Valley Scrappers, a Cleveland Indians Single-A affiliate, played right next door in Niles for over two decades before the great minor league contraction of 2021 wiped them off the map. There is something deeply on-brand about the Whistlepigs moving to a city whose baseball team no longer exists.

But the true historical parallel belongs to the 1899 Cleveland Spiders. When that team's owners purchased the St. Louis Browns franchise, they promptly stripped Cleveland of every good player and shipped them south, leaving behind a skeleton squad so bad they went 20-134 — the worst single-season record in professional baseball history. Unable to sell tickets at home, they were forced to play most games on the road, where local papers dubbed them 'The Wanderers' and 'The Misfits.' They finished the season by wandering from city to city, losing to everyone, welcomed nowhere.

The Whistlepigs are not the 1899 Spiders. They went 12-10 in 2024 and show genuine promise. They have simply, in four seasons of trying, not yet made the playoffs. Ohio has seen worse. Ohio has seen much worse. Welcome to the Mahoning Valley, Luke. There is history here, and iron ore, and a burning competitive desire to someday finish above .500 in October. You'll fit right in.
